results.title: DK Shivakumar Meets Karnataka Governor, To Take CM Oath On June 3
results.description: Karnataka Congress president D K Shivakumar has staked claim to form the government after being unanimously elected leader of the Congress Legislature Party (CLP). Shivakumar met Governor Thaawarchand Gehlot at Raj Bhavan shortly after the CLP meeting at Vidhana Soudha in Bengaluru. Outgoing chief minister Siddaramaiah proposed Shivakumar’s name, which was seconded by senior leader G. Parameshwara and unanimously approved by party legislators. The meeting was overseen by Congress leaders KC Venugopal and Randeep Singh Surjewala. The CLP also passed a resolution appreciating Siddaramaiah’s tenure as chief minister. Following Siddaramaiah’s resignation and the dissolution of the Council of Ministers, Shivakumar is now set to take oath as Karnataka’s next chief minister on June 3 in Bengaluru.

results.title: Give MAGA a try' | US escalates war of words with Lamola
results.description: Relations between South Africa and the United States have deteriorated further after the US State Department hit back at Ronald Lamola, citing unemployment and saying SA should “give MAGA a try”.

results.title: UN Report Raises Alarm Over Conflict-Related Sexual Violence
results.description:
New York (HRNW) — The United Nations has released a major report warning of a rise in conflict-related sexual violence in several war-affected regions. According to the report presented by António Guterres, verified cases involve women, men, and children in areas affected by armed conflict. The UN said sexual violence continues to be used as a...

The post UN Report Raises Alarm Over Conflict-Related Sexual Violence first appeared on Human Rights News Worldwide .
results.source: Human Rights News Worldwide Hrnw News Agency

results.title: Dope test to be mandatory before appointment in government service: Himachal CM Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu
results.description: Sukhvinder Singh Sukhu directed all departments to make a dope test mandatory for candidates prior to their appointment in government service and asked them to issue the necessary guidelines for this purpose.

results.title: Ousted Turkish opposition leader Ozel draws thousands in Ankara protest
results.description: By Ece Toksabay ANKARA, May 30 (Reuters) - Turkey's ousted opposition leader Ozgur Ozel showed he can still command strong support on Saturday, drawing a crowd of thousands in Ankara despite a court ruling that removed him from office and dealt a blow to President Tayyip Erdogan's challengers. After addressing supporters - estimated in the tens of thousands by the private Anka news agency and media outlets close to the opposition - Ozel led a march through the capital following last week's ruling that removed him as leader of the main opposition CHP.
